---
title: "Canon Canonical Rules"
subtitle: "Normative rules governing UIAO document structure, IDs, and validity"
uiao:
  id: UIAO_META_CANON_RULES
  status: Current
  owner: Governance Board
  tags: [governance, meta, architecture]
  created: "2026-05-06"
  lastUpdated: "2026-05-06"
summary: >
  The normative rules that all UIAO canon documents must follow.
  Covers document ID patterns, status lifecycle, required frontmatter fields,
  CI validation rules, and naming conventions. Immutable except via ADR.
fabric: governance
---

# Canon Canonical Rules

These rules are **immutable except via ADR** and apply to every document in the UIAO canon.
CI validation enforces required fields, allowed values, and naming patterns on every merge.

## Document classes

The UIAO canon contains two document classes, each with its own metadata contract:

| Class | Schema | Location |
|-------|--------|----------|
| **Standard documents** — appendices, governance docs, contributor guides, dashboards | [UIAO Document Metadata Contract](../meta/uiao-document-metadata-contract.qmd) | `docs/` tree |
| **Architecture Decision Records** | [ADR Metadata Contract](../meta/uiao-adr-metadata-contract.qmd) | `src/uiao/canon/adr/` |

---

## Rule 1 — Document IDs

Every UIAO document must have a stable canonical ID (`uiao.id`) that uniquely identifies it
across the entire corpus. The ID is the cross-reference key used by ADRs, dashboards, and
the CI validation pipeline.

**Pattern for standard documents:** `UIAO_<AppendixLetter>_<Number>`

| Example | Document |
|---------|----------|
| `UIAO_A_01` | Appendix A, document 1 (Adapter Lifecycle) |
| `UIAO_E_03` | Appendix E (Governance Plane), document 3 |
| `UIAO_META_DOC_01` | Meta contract (non-appendix documents use descriptive segment) |
| `UIAO_CANON_01` | Canon overview |

**Pattern for ADRs:** `UIAO_ADR_<Number>` — e.g., `UIAO_ADR_012`.

**Rules:**
- IDs are permanent. Once assigned, an ID never changes even if the document is superseded.
- Duplicate IDs are prohibited and will block CI.
- The `uiao.id` in frontmatter must match the document's registration in `document-registry.yaml`.

---

## Rule 2 — Status lifecycle

Every document has a `uiao.status` field that describes its lifecycle position:

| Status | Meaning | Who may set it |
|--------|---------|---------------|
| `Current` | Authoritative and up to date | Governance Board on merge |
| `Draft` | Under active development, not yet binding | Author |
| `Needs Replacing` | Outdated; rewrite required before use | Governance Board |
| `Needs Creating` | Placeholder exists, content missing | Author |
| `Deprecated` | Superseded or retired, kept for lineage | Governance Board |

**Transition rules:**
- `Draft` → `Current` requires a merge approval from the Governance Board.
- `Current` → `Needs Replacing` is set by the Governance Board when a document is materially outdated.
- `Current` → `Deprecated` requires an ADR that names the superseding document.
- Direct `Draft` → `Deprecated` is allowed for documents that were never published.

---

## Rule 3 — Required frontmatter fields

Every standard UIAO document must include these fields in the YAML front matter:

```yaml
---
title: <Human-readable title>      # Must match the H1 heading exactly

uiao:
  id: UIAO_<Segment>_<Number>      # Canonical ID — see Rule 1
  status: Current                  # Status lifecycle — see Rule 2
  owner: team@example.com          # Accountable owner (team or role)
  tags:                            # At least one canonical tag
    - governance
  created: 2026-04-07              # ISO date — date of creation
  lastUpdated: 2026-04-07          # ISO date — updated on every merge
  commit: a1b2c3d                  # Short git hash — CI updates on merge

summary: >                         # 1-3 sentence description of scope
  Short description.
---
```

**Optional but recommended:**

```yaml
version: 1.0          # Semantic version for stable documents
fabric: governance    # Primary fabric (identity, drift, evidence, adapter, governance)
```

---

## Rule 4 — Title-heading alignment

The `title` field in frontmatter must exactly match the H1 heading (`# Title`) in the document body.
CI validation checks this on every commit. Mismatches block merge.

---

## Rule 5 — Tag vocabulary

The `uiao.tags` field uses a closed vocabulary. Allowed values:

| Tag | Scope |
|-----|-------|
| `identity` | Identity plane documents |
| `drift` | Drift detection and management |
| `evidence` | Evidence pipeline and packaging |
| `adapter` | Adapter plane documents |
| `governance` | Governance OS and policy |
| `meta` | Canon infrastructure and contracts |
| `architecture` | Architectural reference documents |
| `operations` | Operational runbooks and procedures |
| `compliance` | FedRAMP, CISA, SCuBA compliance documents |

Multiple tags are permitted. At least one is required.

---

## Rule 6 — Provenance commit hash

The `uiao.commit` field records the short git hash of the commit that introduced the current
version of the document. This field enables the corpus integrity verification pipeline to detect
documents that have been edited outside the normal PR process.

CI automatically updates `uiao.commit` on merge. Authors do not need to set it manually,
but a placeholder must be present:

```yaml
uiao:
  commit: <short-hash>     # CI updates this on merge
```

---

## Rule 7 — Prohibited patterns

The following patterns are explicitly prohibited and will block CI:

- **Duplicate `uiao.id`** across any two documents in the corpus.
- **Missing status badge** in the top 8 lines of the rendered document.
- **Missing provenance footer** (the `> SSOT Reference` block at the document end).
- **`title` / H1 mismatch** — frontmatter title does not exactly match the H1.
- **Undefined tag values** — tags outside the closed vocabulary in Rule 5.
- **Future `lastUpdated` dates** — dates after the commit timestamp.

---

## Rule 8 — ADR-only changes to this document

This document (`UIAO_META_CANON_RULES`) is immutable except via Architecture Decision Record.
Any change to the rules above — including adding new status values, new tag vocabulary,
or modifying the ID pattern — requires a formally accepted ADR that references this document
in its `affects:` field.

---

## See also

- [UIAO Document Metadata Contract](../meta/uiao-document-metadata-contract.qmd) — full frontmatter schema with examples
- [ADR Metadata Contract](../meta/uiao-adr-metadata-contract.qmd) — ADR-specific schema
- [Corpus Status Dashboard](corpus-status-dashboard.qmd) — current compliance state across the corpus
- [ADR Index](../adr/index.qmd) — all binding architectural decisions
